step_by_step_thinking:
The patient is presenting with various symptoms that could indicate heart conditions, particularly those associated with shunt defects like Eisenmenger syndrome. In this condition, right-to-left shunting leads to increased pulmonary vascular resistance over time. 

The correct answer would be B: Single S2, as it does not typically occur in patients with Eisenmenger syndrome; instead, you'd expect splitting or fixed split S2 due to altered blood flow dynamics. A: Increased flow murmurs suggest ongoing left-to-right shunts characteristic early on but may persist if there's significant regurgitation later. C: Loud P2 indicates high pulmonary pressures which can accompany Eisenmenger changes. D: Graham-Steel murmur reflects left-sided volume overload secondary to severe right ventricular failure typical of advanced Eisenmenger disease. Answer Choice B (Single S2).
raw_response:
The patient is presenting with various symptoms that could indicate heart conditions, particularly those associated with shunt defects like Eisenmenger syndrome. In this condition, right-to-left shunting leads to increased pulmonary vascular resistance over time. 

The correct answer would be B: Single S2, as it does not typically occur in patients with Eisenmenger syndrome; instead, you'd expect splitting or fixed split S2 due to altered blood flow dynamics. A: Increased flow murmurs suggest ongoing left-to-right shunts characteristic early on but may persist if there's significant regurgitation later. C: Loud P2 indicates high pulmonary pressures which can accompany Eisenmenger changes. D: Graham-Steel murmur reflects left-sided volume overload secondary to severe right ventricular failure typical of advanced Eisenmenger disease. Answer Choice B (Single S2).
